reviews.review_description: I started just learning then found there is a game underneath and behind the library. Some how I got through a wall and there it was. Cool.
It no longer works. Anyone who bought it wasted their money, like me. Meta should compensate, but they won't.

reviews.review_title: What did I just pay for?
reviews.review_description: First of all, I can’t skip past the introduction and second of all whoever developed this app needs to learn how properly develop an application before publishing the application to the public. I tried to hop in twice already, and somehow I landed myself outside of the library and couldn’t get back in. come on now people are tight on money. Please do better than this. Nobody wants to throw money at a soft handed developer. ￼￼￼

Bought this a year ago to help my kids study.  The only thing we have been able to do is tour the library.  So basically $9.99 to walk around an empty library.  There have been no updates or fixes and it's been this way for the entire year.
Overall, was a pretty good game, but major connection issues that make the game literally unplayable, the game won’t leave the log in page.

I’m sorry, wanted to like it. Joined the discord and it’s just random chit chat…bought the game the day it was released.

Waited until now to review, giving developers a chance to improve it.

Good idea, poor execution. Willing to revise my review in the future if there are any changes though.

Best of luck to the developers, but I don’t recommend spending your money on this.

Edit 2024: Abandoned. Developers didn’t try to improve it. Don’t buy.

Such a good product for learning. We emailed support offering to buy this entire product so we can continue developing, as we really believe in this. They didn’t respond for months.  I support. Just left on store. Typical of many projects. Starts initially out of passion, but the fire is out out.

My life is messy. Librarium helped me study in a new space when I had little available to me. It had things that were annoying such as the cards vanishing and the difficulty to add new content. I thought it would be fixed by now. It's been a year. I'm frustrated at the game for not furthering the quality of their product. I would pay for an update at this point
Tried to register app won't move past it
It won't let you enter without registration, but it won't let you register either

Broken app clearly has a long way to go.
You learn how to grab an apple and teleport. You literally can't do anything else. What a waste of $10 for what? To walk around an empty library with none of the features advertised because something's clearly unfixed? I teleported literally everywhere and grabbed all the cards but no progress. Exited and returned multiple times, same thing. Octopus is dead silent and I keep getting the teleport tooltip even after using it a hundred times.

Also don't understand why an app called Librarium doesnt already have a feature to read an e-book. Save your money and buy it when it's a REAL app. They can raise their own money through liabilities or equity if they need it that badly, but save your money while it's still trash. They will take your money and give you nothing in return.

The subject says it all. You should be banned from making apps. t
This needs to be removed immediately.

So I pick up the deck I want to use, throw it in the air as instructed and it disappears into a puff of smoke,  nothing happens. Tried tutorial, tried my own deck, same thing, broken, don’t buy until fixed.

The concept is great, it helps you develop your memory with one of many methods. Personally, I appreciate it! I think that those who don’t like it comes from the problem that he expects a game. Here it’s more productivity than he thinks. If you want to play, it’s not too indicated. If you are a student and want to develop your thesis or even a professional who has trouble remembering these presentations then do not hesitate. For developers I think it would be great if you presented a kind of sandbox a bit like other developers do in population 1 or others, to allow players to create their own palace according to their preferred environment. I’m sure that a lot would pay for this in extension. First offer a free period in beta and formalize 1 or 2 months later. And Please add french langage To have more users, or put a translated subtitle option if it’s too hard And it would be excellent if at the beginning of each game the application asks if we play standing or sitting so as to adapt the heights to the players, several times I was standing but my feet crossed the ground, not cool! I’m coming to change my mind there’s a bug my cards don’t all appear in the application

This game is truly unique and so imaginative. The library feels like a magical place to study, browse community decks to learn new topics, and explore new areas with puzzle solving. The interactions feel smooth, comfortable, and well designed. And the subtle references to other fantasy archetypes and images really nicely plays into the memory palace concept.  You can tell the creators truly care about learning and their community members. Would highly recommend!

I've just been going through the Community decks and though these are not quite what I need there are still some interesting subjects. What I'm interested in is the building of your own decks and then placing the icons within your own organized sections. I've been watching a video on Youtube by a guy named Mac Hyndman where he has done his own organizing into a grid in a small area. And, even now, I can picture the structure and with the icons (for the 12 labours of Hercules) and the information behind the cue cards. Awesome.  When I do this myself and put subject grids all over the environment this will work brilliantly. Can't wait to figure this out.

reviews.review_title: Incredible! I just have one suggestion
reviews.review_description: I loved the app, it really sets the mood to study. Nevertheless I believe it can be useful to create our custom index cards in something we can access from the computer since typing index cards in the phone can be exhausting. Other than that, I'm truly happu

developer_response.body:
Thank you! So happy to hear that Librarium is useful to you! 
We are working hard to launch a web version of the content creator soon!

reviews.review_title: Good idea needs a way to import pictures
reviews.review_description: This is a neat concept to associate concepts with objects using flash cards in a neat environment. It is time consuming to make your flash cards but that's half of the learning. I would however like a way to import pictures for the flash cards. I usually study electronics and some of the circuitry is hard to convey using just words. This would also be helpful for stuff like biological classification of species. Will update rating if that becomes available in future versions.

developer_response.body: Great minds think alike! This is on our roadmap and we have some work on our side to make sure the experience supports it reliably. Thanks for the suggestion and the support!

reviews.review_description:
An intriguing way to learn but as a handicap with mobility issues, retrieving flashcards on top of shelves is deemed a Herculean task. If the gameplay could be adjusted to attune disabled persons, that would be great! Thank you!

18/1 downgraded from 3★→1★ simply because of lousy customer service. My emails have been ignored 2 times in a row! Disappointed but too late to ask for a refund! If you are a handicap like me, think thrice before falling into this trap of no return.

2/2 With sincerity and apologies, the Librarium team listened with all ears and offered meticulous help that has eased my gameplay and now I just can't stop revising &  learning new materials daily. Therefore 1★→4★

developer_response.body: Thank you Aqua, you have been awesome and we are grateful to have you as a part of our community!

reviews.review_title: Good Idea but tedious and unengaging
reviews.review_description:
I like the concept. The idea is intriguing. But it isn't fun. The movement controls are clunky, searching for items is pretty tedious just a little underwhelmed by the whole experience.

If it was just OK I'd probably use it but if it's controls, design and execution annoy me them I'm going to avoid it as it will link study with annoyance in my memory.

Nice idea but not good enough to be useful yet.

developer_response.body:
Hello B0ttlebank!

We're glad to hear you like our general direction, and as teaching methods evolve, so does Librarium! We appreciate and value your input on our controls and overall experience. We will work hard on improving and making them more accessible. We would really appreciate if you could send any additional feedback to support@librarium-vr.com.
